title: US-Israel Strikes on Iran Hit Neighbouring Countries | 10 News
description: Neighboring countries have been caught in the joint US-Israel operation against Iran with strikes reported in Bahrain, Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Doha, and Riyadh.  It comes amid claims Iran’s Supreme Leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, has been killed as well as a number of senior Iranian leaders have died.  Meanwhile, major airports, including Dubai International Airport and Doha Airport, have been affected, leaving thousands of travellers stranded across the region. title: Explosions Erupt Across Middle East As US-Israel Strikes Kill Iran’s Supreme Leader | 10 News
description: Explosions continue across the Middle East nearly a day after Israel and the United States launched joint strikes on Iran, killing hundreds, including the country’s Supreme Leader.  The first attacks hit just before 9:30 a.m. local time, with blasts reported across Tehran and other areas. A primary school near a military target was reportedly struck, leaving dozens dead.  Iran responded swiftly, firing on regional US military bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, Qatar, and the UAE.  The strikes have disrupted global travel, leaving Australians among thousands stranded abroad. title: Airports Bombed, Aussies Left Scrambling, Iran's Supreme Leader Dead, Trump's Warning | 10 News+
description: Iran has confirmed the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei after a massive US-Israeli attack that US President Donald Trump said would continue with heavy bombing if Iran continues to retaliate.  Iran is striking back with a fresh wave of attacks across the region after the supreme leader’s killing. It has already unleashed strikes on US military bases, Israel and targets across the Middle East, damaging air hubs, rocking densely populated areas and disrupting oil shipments.  The contrast of celebrations and mourning highlights deep divides in Iran. Across the world, people took to the streets, with some celebrating and others protesting the strikes on Iran.  While Australians have been warned not to travel to Iran since 2020, the federal government has now extended that advice for more countries caught up in the firing line.   10 News+ LIVE: Trusted, in-depth reporting from Australia and around the world. 👍 Like this stream to support our in-depth coverage 🔔 Subscribe and turn on notifications for breaking news alerts ↗ Share this coverage to keep others informed  📲 Stay Across Breaking News 24/7 Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/10NewsPlus/ TikTok: https://www.tiktok.com/@10newsplus/ Snapchat: https://www.snapchat.com/add/tennewsplus 🎧 Listen on Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/0tWZ6sIJTQ2WHgYdjgaAA7?si=cb30d7fd3d3541fa  📺 Watch 10 News+ 6pm Monday–Friday | 6:30pm Sunday on Network 10 Missed the bulletin? title: UK To Permit US To Use Its Bases To Strike Iran | 10 News
description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has confirmed he has permitted the United States to use UK bases in the Middle East for the purposes of launching further strikes on Iran.  Facing internal criticism for not permitting the Trump Administration from using them sooner, Starmer confirmed the move in a statement posted to social media on Sunday night London time.  More to come. title: Trump Confirms Three US Service Members Killed In Iran Operation | 10 News
description: US President Donald Trump has confirmed that three American service members have been killed in action and five seriously wounded as part of combat operations in Iran.  Trump said in a video posted on Truth Social that Operation Epic Fury was continuing in Iran and would carry on until all of Washington's objectives are achieved, vowing to avenge the deaths of Americans.  The US and Israel attack on Iran has disrupted flights across the Middle East as countries around the region closed their airspace and several key connecting airports remain closed on Sunday as the conflict moved into its second day. title: FBI Probes Possible Terrorism Link To Fatal Austin Shooting | 10 News
description: The FBI are investigating a late-night shooting in Austin, with investigators saying there were "indicators" suggesting a "potential nexus to terrorism" related to the shooter and his vehicle.  Three people were killed when the gunman opened fire from inside a vehicle in front of a bar, 14 further were injured, three of which are critical.  Police are yet to release the identity of the shooter or make further comment on the shooter's motive.  Law enforcement across the United States are on high alert after the US and Israel opened a campaign of airstrikes against Iran on Saturday, killing its supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
